title: hypericum crux-andreae
intro: Hypericum crux-andreae, also known as Cross-of-St-Andrew or Tetrapteris, is a species of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its hyperforin content. The health benefits associated with Hypericum crux-andreae include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve mood, and aid in wound healing. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion, tincture, or ointment, and can be used topically or internally, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
